42.19: If you are honest people, let one of you, your brother, remain bound in your prison, and you, go, take some grain with you calm the hunger of your families.
42.20: And bring me your youngest brother, and your words will be proven true, and you will not die. And they did so.
42.21: Then they said to one another, “We are truly being punished because of our brother. We saw the anguish of his soul when he begged for mercy, but we would not listen to him; therefore this distress has come upon us.”.
42.22: Reuben, speaking up, said to them: Did I not tell you: Do not sin against the child? And you did not listen, and now his blood is being demanded.
42.23: They did not know that Joseph understood, because they were speaking to him through an interpreter.
42.24: And he turned away from them and wept. Being Next He returned to them and spoke to them, and he took Simeon from among them and had him bound before their eyes.
42.25: Then Joseph commanded that their ships be filled with grain, that each man's money be put back in his sack, and that they be given provisions for the journey. And it was done to them.
42.26: Having loaded the wheat onto their donkeys, they set off.
42.27: At the place where they spent the night, one of them opened his bag to give fodder to his donkey, and he saw his money, which was at the entrance of the bag.
42.28: He said to his brothers, "They have handed over my money; here it is in my bag." And their hearts failed them, and they said to one another, trembling, "What has God done to us?"
42.29: They returned to Jacob, their father, in the land of Canaan, and told him all that had happened to them, saying:
42.30: The man who is the master of the country spoke harshly to us and took us for people spying on the country.
42.31: We told him: We are honest people, we are not spies.
42.32: We are twelve brothers, sons of the same father, one is no more, and the youngest is now with our father in the land of Canaan.
42.33: And the man who is master of the land said to us, “By this I will know that you are honest people: leave one of you, your brother, with me, and take…” something to calm things down Leave your families hungry and go away.,
42.34: And bring me your youngest brother. I will know Thus that you are not spies, but honest people. I will then return your brother to you and you will be able to trade in the country.
42.35: As they emptied their bags, each man's bundle of money was in his bag. They and their father saw their bundles of money, and they were frightened.
42.36: Jacob, their father, said to them: You have left me childless; Joseph is no more, Simeon is no more, and you are going to take Benjamin. All this falls on me.
42.37: Ruben said to his father: You will kill my two sons if I do not bring Benjamin back to you; put him in my hands, and I will bring him back to you.
42.38: He said, “My son will not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he alone remains. If harm should befall him on the journey you are about to make, you would send my gray hairs down in sorrow to the realm of the dead.”.

43.1: Famine was weighing heavily on the country.
43.2: When they had finished eating the wheat they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, “Go back and buy us some more food.”.
43.3: Judah replied: This man has given us this solemn declaration: You will not see my face unless your brother is with you.
43.4: If you let our brother come with us, we will go down and buy you some food.
43.5: But if you do not let him come, we will not go down, for this man told us: You will not see my face unless your brother is with you.
43.6: And Israel said, “Why have you caused me this grief by telling this man that you still had a brother?”
43.7: They said, "This man asked us many questions about ourselves and our family, saying, 'Is your father still alive? Do you have a other Brother? And we answered according to these questions. Could we have known that he would say: Bring your brother down?
43.8: And Judah said to Israel his father, “Let the child go with me, so that we may get up and go on a journey, and we shall live and not die, we, you and our little ones.”.
43.9: I am responsible for him; you will demand him back from me. If I do not bring him back to you, if I do not place him before you, I will be guilty towards you forever.
43.10: Because if we hadn't so much If it had been delayed, we would now be back twice.
43.11: Israel, their father, said to them: Well, since it must be so, do this: Take some of the best produce of the land from your baggage and bring this man a present: a little balm and a little honey, astragalus, ladanum, pistachios and almonds.
43.12: Take duplicate money in your hands, and return the money that was placed at the entrance of your bags, perhaps by mistake.
43.13: Take your brother, get up and go back to that man.
43.14: May Almighty God grant you mercy before this man, so that he will let your other brother and Benjamin return with you. As for me, if I must be bereaved of my children, so be it.
43.15: The men took this gift, and they took double the amount of silver in their hands, as did Benjamin, and they got up and went down to Egypt and presented themselves before Joseph.
43.16: As soon as Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to his steward: Bring these men into the house, kill victims and prepares a meal , because these people will eat with me at noon.
43.17: This man did as Joseph had commanded, and he led these people into Joseph's house.
43.18: While they were being led to Joseph's house, the men were afraid, and they said: It is because of the money brought back in our bags the other day that we are being taken away, it is to attack us, to fall upon us, to take us as slaves with our donkeys.
43.19: Having approached the steward of Joseph's house, they addressed him at the entrance of the house,
43.20: saying: Pardon, my lord. We already went down once to buy food.
43.21: On the return trip, When we arrived at the place where we were to spend the night, we opened our bags, and behold, each man's money was at the mouth of his bag, our money according to its weight: we are bringing it back with us,
43.22: And at the same time we brought more money to buy food. We don't know who put our money in our bags.
43.23: He said to them, “Peace be with you. Do not be afraid. It is your God, the God of your father, who has given you treasure in your sacks. Your money has been delivered to me.” And he brought Simeon to them.
43.24: This man brought them into Joseph's house, gave them water and they washed their feet, and he also gave fodder to their donkeys.
43.25: They prepared their gift, while waiting for Joseph to come at noon, for they had been told that they would eat at his house.
43.26: When Joseph arrived home, they brought him into the house the gift they had in their hand, and bowed down on the ground before him.
43.27: He asked them how they were doing, then he said: Your old father, whom you spoke of, is he in good health? Is he still alive?
43.28: They answered, "Your servant, our father, is well; he is still alive," and they bowed down and prostrated themselves.
43.29: Joseph He looked up and saw Benjamin, his brother, his mother's son. He said, "Is this your younger brother about whom you spoke?" And he said, "May God be gracious to you, my son.".
43.30: So, in great haste, for his heart was moved for his brother, Joseph sought a place To cry, he went into his room and cried there.
43.31: After washing his face, he went out and, making an effort to restrain himself, said: Serve the food.
43.32: He was served separately, and his brothers separately, and also the Egyptians who ate with him, for the Egyptians may not eat with the Hebrews: it is an abomination to the Egyptians.
43.33: Joseph's brothers They sat before him, the firstborn according to his birthright, and the youngest according to his age, and they looked at one another in amazement.
43.34: He had portions brought to them from before him, and Benjamin's portion was five times larger than all of their portions combined. They drank joyfully with him.

44.1: Joseph gave this order to the steward of his house: Fill these men's sacks with as much food as they can carry, and put each man's money at the opening of his sack.
44.2: You shall also put my cup, the silver cup, in the mouth of the younger son's sack, along with the money for his grain. The steward did as Joseph had instructed him.
44.3: In the morning, as soon as it was light, the men were sent back with their donkeys.
44.4: They had left the city, but were not yet far away, when Joseph said to his steward, "Get up, pursue those people, and when you catch them, say to them, 'Why have you repaid evil for good?'"
44.5: Isn't that right? the cup in which my lord drinks, and which he uses for divination? This is a bad deed you have done.
44.6: The steward, having joined them, said the same words to them.
44.7: They replied, "Why does my lord speak like this? May God protect your servants from committing such an act.".
44.8: Behold, we brought you silver from the land of Canaan, which we found in the mouths of our sacks; how could we have stolen silver or gold from the house of your lord?
44.9: Let the one among your servants on whom is found the cup die, and that we ourselves may be slaves to my lord.
44.10: He their said: Well, let it be according to your words, the one with whom he will be the cup will be my slave, and you will be free of it.
44.11: Each person immediately took their bag down to the ground and each person opened their bag.
44.12: The steward searched them, beginning with the oldest and ending with the youngest, and the cup was found in Benjamin's bag.
44.13: They tore their clothes and, each having reloaded his donkey, they returned to the city.
44.14: Judah and his brothers came to the house of Joseph, who was still there, and they bowed down to the ground before him.
44.15: Joseph said to them, "What have you done? Didn't you know that a man like me would be able to guess?"
44.16: Judah replied, “What can we say to my lord? How can we speak? How can we defend ourselves? God has found the iniquity of your servants. We are slaves of my lord, we and the one in whose possession the cup was found.”.
44.17: And Joseph said, God forbid that I should do this; the man in whose possession the cup was found shall be my slave, and you go up in peace to your father.
44.18: Then Judah approached Joseph, him He said: Please, my lord, let your servant speak a word in the hearing of my lord, and do not let your anger burn against your servant, for you are equal to Pharaoh.
44.19: My lord questioned his servants, saying: Do you have a father or a brother?.
44.20: And we answered my lord: We have an old father and a young brother, the child of his old age, this child had a brother who died, and he remains the only one of the same mother, and his father loves him.
44.21: You said to your servants: Bring him down to me, so that I may look upon him.
44.22: We replied to my lord: The child cannot leave his father; if he leaves him, his father will die.
44.23: You said to your servants: If your youngest brother does not come down with you, you will never see my face again.
44.24: When we went back to your servant, my father, we reported to him the words of my lord.
44.25: And when our father said, "Go back and buy us some food,",
44.26: We replied: We cannot go down, but if our youngest brother is with us, we will go down, for we cannot see the face of this man unless our youngest brother is with us.
44.27: Your servant, our father, told us: You know that my wife has borne me two sons.
44.28: One of them went away from me, and I said, 'He must have been eaten, for I have not seen him again until now.'.
44.29: If you take this one from me again and something bad happens to him, you will send my white hair down in sorrow to the realm of the dead.
44.30: Now, when I return to your servant, my father, without the child with us, to whose soul his soul is bound,
44.31: As soon as he sees that the child is not there, he will die, and your servants will have brought down in sorrow to the grave the gray hair of your servant, our father.
44.32: For your servant has answered for the child by taking it To my father, he said: If I do not bring him back to you, I will be guilty before my father forever.
44.33: Please allow me, then, that Me , your servant, I remain in the place of the child as a slave of my lord, and let the child go back up with his brothers.
44.34: How could I go back to my father if the child is not with me? No, I must not see the affliction that would overwhelm my father.

45.1: Then Joseph could not contain himself in front of all those present, and he cried out, “Have everyone leave!” And no one remained with him when he revealed himself to his brothers.
45.2: He raised his voice in tears, the Egyptians heard it, and the house of Pharaoh heard it.
45.3: Joseph said to his brothers, "I am Joseph. Is my father still alive?" But his brothers could not answer him, so distraught were they in his presence.
45.4: And Joseph said to his brothers, “Come here to me,” and they came. He said, “I am Joseph, your brother, whom you sold.” to be led in Egypt.
45.5: Now do not grieve or be angry with yourselves about what you sold to me to be driven this is for save you the life that God has sent me before you.
45.6: For two years now there has been famine in this country, and for five more years there will be neither plowing nor harvesting.
45.7: God has sent me ahead of you to ensure a remnant for you in the land and to preserve you for a great deliverance.
45.8: And now, it is not you who sent me here, but God; he has appointed me father to Pharaoh, lord over all his house and ruler over all the land of Egypt.
45.9: Hurry up to my father, and tell him: This is what your son Joseph says: God has made me lord over all Egypt, come down to me without delay.
45.10: You will live in the land of Gessen, and you will be near me, you and your sons, and your sons' sons, your sheep and your cattle, and all that is yours.
45.11: There I will feed you, for there will be five more years of famine, so that you will not suffer, you and your household and all that you have.
45.12: Behold, your eyes see, and the eyes of my brother Benjamin, that it is my mouth that speaks to you.
45.13: Tell my father about all my glory in Egypt and everything you saw, and bring my father down here as soon as possible.
45.14: SO He threw himself on the neck of Benjamin, his brother, and wept, and Benjamin wept on his neck.
45.15: He embrace also to all his brothers, and wept holding them embraced , Then his brothers spoke with him.
45.16: The news spread in Pharaoh's house that Joseph's brothers had come: this pleased Pharaoh and his servants.
45.17: And Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Tell your brothers, ‘Do this: load your animals and go to the land of Canaan,’”,
45.18: And, having taken your father and your families, return to me. I will give you the best of the land of Egypt, and you will eat the fat of the land.
45.19: You are authorized to tell them: Do this: take wagons from the land of Egypt for your children and for your wives, bring your father and come.
45.20: Do not let your eyes linger with regret on the objects that you will have to leave , because the best that the whole country of Egypt has to offer is at your disposal.
45.21: The Israelites did so, and Joseph gave them wagons, according to Pharaoh's command, as well as provisions for the journey.
45.22: He gave each of them a change of clothes, and he gave Benjamin three hundred pieces of silver and five changes of clothes.
45.23: He also sent his father ten donkeys loaded with the best produce of Egypt, and ten female donkeys loaded with wheat, bread, and provisions, for his father during the journey.
45.24: Then he dismissed his brothers, who departed, and he said to them, “Do not quarrel on the way.”.
45.25: Having come up from Egypt, they arrived in the land of Canaan, to Jacob, their father.
45.26: They told him, "Joseph is still alive; indeed, he is ruling over all the land of Egypt." But his heart remained cold, because he did not believe them.
45.27: They then told him everything Joseph had said. When he saw the wagons Joseph had sent to transport him, the spirit of Jacob, their father, revived.,
45.28: And Israel said, "That is enough. Joseph, my son, still lives. I will go and see him before I die.".

46.1: Israel set out with all that belonged to him. Arriving at Beersheba, he offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac.
46.2: And God spoke to Israel in a vision of the night, and said: Jacob, Jacob. Israel answered: Here I am.
46.3: And God said: I am the mighty God, the God of your father. Do not be afraid to go down to Egypt, for there I will make you into a great nation.
46.4: I myself will go down with you to Egypt, and I myself will surely bring you back up from there, and Joseph will lay his hand on your eyes.
46.5: Jacob arose and left Beersheba, and the sons of Israel put Jacob their father, and their wives and children, on the chariots which Pharaoh had sent to transport him.
46.6: They also took their flocks and possessions that they had acquired in the land of Canaan. And Jacob went to Egypt with all his family.
46.7: He took with him to Egypt his sons and his sons' sons, his daughters and his sons' daughters, and all his family.
46.8: These are the names of the sons of Israel who came to Egypt: Jacob and his sons. Jacob's firstborn, Reuben.
46.9: Reuben's sons: Enoch, Phallu, Hesron and Charmi.
46.10: The sons of Simeon: Jamuel, Jamin, Ahod, Jachin and Zohar, and Saul, son of the Canaanite woman.
46.11: Levi's sons: Gerson, Caat, and Merari.
46.12: The sons of Judah were Her, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah, but Her and Onan had died in the land of Canaan. The sons of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
46.13: The sons of Issachar: Tola, Puah, Job, and Shemron.
46.14: Sons of Zebulun: Sared, Elon and Jahelel.
46.15: These were the sons that Leah bore to Jacob in Paddan-Aram, with her daughter Dinah. Her sons and daughters were thirty-three in all.
46.16: Sons of Gad: Sephion, Haggi, Suni, Esebon, Heri, Arodi and Areli.
46.17: The sons of Asher: Jamneh, Jeshua, Jessui, and Beriah, and Sarah their sister. The sons of Beriah were Heber and Melchiel.
46.18: These were the sons of Zelpah, whom Laban had given to his daughter Leah, and she bore them to Jacob: in all sixteen people.
46.19: The sons of Rachel, Jacob's wife: Joseph and Benjamin.
46.20: He was born to Joseph in the land of Egypt, threads that Aseneth, daughter of Potiphar, priest of On, bore to him, know Manasseh and Ephraim.
46.21: Sons of Benjamin: Bela, Bochor, Asbel, Gera, Naaman, Echi, Ros, Mophim, Ophim and Ared.
46.22: These were the sons of Rachel, who were born to Jacob: fourteen persons in all.
46.24: Sons of Naphtali: Jasiel, Guni, Jeser and Salem.
46.25: These were the sons of Balaam, whom Laban had given to Rachel, his daughter, and she bore them to Jacob: seven persons in all.
46.26: All the people who came with Jacob to Egypt, descended from him, besides the wives of Jacob’s sons, were sixty-six in all.
46.27: Joseph had two sons born to him in Egypt. The total number of people from Jacob's family who came to Egypt was seventy.
46.28: Jacob had sent Judah ahead of him to Joseph to prepare for his arrival in Geshen. When Jacob and his family had entered Geshen,
46.29: Joseph had his chariot harnessed and mounted it, to go to Geshen to meet Israel, his father. He appeared to him and, throwing himself on his neck, wept for a long time upon his neck.
46.30: Israel said to Joseph, "I can now die, since I have seen your face and you are still alive.".
46.31: Joseph said to his brothers and to his father’s family, “I will go and tell Pharaoh, ‘My brothers and my father’s family, who were in the land of Canaan, have come to me.
46.32: These men are herding sheep, for they are owners of flocks; they have brought their sheep and their cattle, and all that belongs to them.
46.33: And when Pharaoh calls you and says, "What is your occupation?"
46.34: You will answer: We, your servants, have been owners of flocks from our youth until now, as were our fathers. In this way you will dwell in the land of Gessen, for all shepherds are an abomination to the Egyptians.

47.1: Joseph went and told Pharaoh, saying, “My father and my brothers have come from the land of Canaan with their sheep and cattle and all that belongs to them, and here they are in the land of Geshen.”.
47.2: Having taken five of his brothers, he presented them to Pharaoh,
47.3: And Pharaoh said to them, “What is your occupation?” They answered Pharaoh, “We, your servants, are shepherds, like were our fathers.
47.4: They also said to Pharaoh, “We have come to sojourn in the land, for there is no more pasture for your servants’ flocks, since the famine has become severe in the land of Canaan. Therefore, please allow your servants to dwell in the land of Geshen.”.
47.5: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “Your father and brothers have come to you. The land of Egypt is before you; settle your father and brothers in the best part of the land.”.
47.6: Let them remain in the land of Gessen, and if you find capable men among them, put them in charge of the flocks that belong to me.
47.7: Joseph brought in his father Jacob and presented him to Pharaoh. Jacob blessed Pharaoh,
47.8: And Pharaoh said to Jacob, "How many days are in the years of your life?"
47.9: Jacob answered Pharaoh: The days of the years of my pilgrimage are one hundred and thirty years. Few and evil have been the days of the years of my life, and they have not attained the days of the years of the life of my fathers during their pilgrimage.
47.10: Jacob blessed Again and withdrew from Pharaoh's presence.
47.11: Joseph settled his father and brothers, and assigned them a property in the land of Egypt, in the best part of the land, in the region of Rameses, as Pharaoh had commanded.
47.12: and Joseph provided bread for his father and his brothers, and all his father's family, according to the number of children.
47.13: There was no more bread in the whole country, for the famine was very great; the land of Egypt and the land of Canaan were exhausted because of the famine.
47.14: Joseph collected all the money that was found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, in exchange for the grain that was bought, and he brought this money into the house of Pharaoh.
47.15: When there was no more money in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan, all the Egyptians came to Joseph, saying, “Give us bread. Why should we die in your presence?” For we are out of money.
47.16: Joseph said, “Bring your flocks, and I will give you bread in exchange for your flocks, since you are out of money.
47.17: They brought their flocks to Joseph, and Joseph gave them bread in exchange for the horses, flocks of sheep and cattle, and donkeys. He provided them Thus bread that year, in exchange for all their herds.
47.18: When that year was over, they came to Joseph the following year and said to him, “We will not hide from my lord that the money is gone and the herds of livestock are gone.” were given To my lord, nothing remains before my lord but our bodies and our lands.
47.19: Why should we and our lands perish before your eyes? Buy us and our lands for bread, and we and our lands will be Pharaoh's slaves, and give us sowing crops so that we may live and not die, and our lands may not be desolate.
47.20: Joseph acquired Thus all the lands of Egypt to Pharaoh, for the Egyptians each sold their field, because the famine pressed upon them, and the land became the property of Pharaoh.
47.21: He led the people through the cities, from one end of the territory of Egypt to the other.
47.22: The only land he did not acquire was the land of the priests, for the priests received a fixed portion from Pharaoh, and they lived on their income which Pharaoh assigned to them: that is why they did not sell their lands.
47.23: Joseph said to the people: I have acquired you today, along with your land, for Pharaoh. Here is some seed for you; sow the land.
47.24: At harvest time, you shall give one fifth to Pharaoh, and you shall have the other four parts to sow your fields and to feed yourselves and those who are in your houses, and to feed your children.
47.25: They said: We owe you our lives. May we find favor with my lord, and we will be Pharaoh's slaves.
47.26: Joseph made this a law, which remains to this day, and by virtue of which the fifth of the produce of the lands of Egypt belongs to Pharaoh, only the lands of the priests do not belong to him.
47.27: Israel lived in the land of Egypt, in the region of Geshen; they acquired possessions there, they were fruitful and multiplied greatly.
47.28: Jacob lived seventeen years in the land of Egypt, and the days of Jacob, the years of his life, were one hundred and forty-seven years.
47.29: When the days of Israel drew to a close, he called his son Joseph and said to him, “If I have found favor in your eyes, put your hand under my thigh and show me kindness and faithfulness: do not bury me in Egypt.”.
47.30: When I am laid to rest with my ancestors, you shall carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their tombs. Joseph replied, “I will do as you say.”.
47.31: And Jacob said, "Swear to me." Joseph swore to him, and Israel bowed down at the head of his bed.

48.1: After these things, Joseph was told, "Your father is sick." He took his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim, with him.
48.2: Jacob was told, "Here is your son Joseph coming to you." Israel gathered his strength and sat up on his bed.
48.3: Jacob said to Joseph, “God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan, and he blessed me.”,
48.4: saying: I will make you fruitful, I will multiply you, and I will make you into a community of peoples, and I will give this land to your descendants after you, to possess forever.
48.5: And now, the two sons who were born to you in the land of Egypt, before I came to you in Egypt, shall be mine, Ephraim and Manasseh shall be mine, as Reuben and Simeon.
48.6: But the children you have fathered after them will be yours; they will be called by the name of their brothers as to their share of the inheritance.
48.7: And I, when I was coming back from Paddan, Rachel died on the way with me, in the land of Canaan, some distance from Ephrath, and there I buried her on the way to Ephrath, which is Bethlehem.
48.8: SO Israel saw the sons of Joseph, and said, Who are these?
48.9: Joseph answered his father, "They are my sons, whom God has given me here." And Israel He said: Please bring them near to me so that I may bless them.
48.10: For the eyes of Israel were dim with age, and they could no longer GOOD See. Joseph brought them near to him, and Israel embraced them, holding them in their embrace.
48.11: And Israel said to Joseph, “I had not expected to see your face again, and now God has shown me your offspring as well.”
48.12: Joseph removed them from between his father's knees and, having bowed down to the ground before him,
48.13: Joseph took them both, Ephraim on his right, on the left of Israel, and Manasseh on his left, on the right of Israel, and he brought them near.
48.14: Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it on the head of Ephraim, who was the younger, and he posed his left hand on Manasseh's head, it was intentionally that he placed his hands in this way, for Manasseh was the elder.
48.15: He blessed Joseph, saying: May the God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, may the God who has nourished me from my existence until this day,
48.16: May the angel who has delivered me from all evil bless these children. May they be called by my name and by the names of my fathers, Abraham and Isaac, and may they multiply abundantly in the midst of the land.
48.17: Joseph, seeing that his father laid his right hand on Ephraim's head, was displeased; he took his father's hand to remove it from Ephraim's head and placed it on Manasseh's head.,
48.18: And Joseph said to his father, “Not so, my father, for this one is the firstborn: put your right hand on his head.”.
48.19: But his father refused, saying: I THE I know, my son, I THE I know, he too will become a people, he too will be great, but his younger brother will be greater than he, and his descendants will become a multitude of nations.
48.20: He blessed them SO that day he said, “Through you Israel will bless, saying, ‘May God make you like Ephraim and Manasseh.’” And he put Ephraim before Manasseh.
48.21: Israel said to Joseph, “Behold, I am about to die. But God will be with you, and he will bring you back to the land of your fathers.”.
48.22: I give you, in addition to your brothers, a portion that I took from the hand of the Amorites with my sword and my bow.

49.1: Jacob called his sons together and said to them, “Gather around, and I will tell you what will happen to you in the last days.”.
49.2: Gather together and listen, sons of Jacob, listen to Israel, your father.
49.3: RUBEN, you are my firstborn, my strength, and the first fruit of my power, superior in dignity and superior in power,
49.4: You boiled like water; you will not have preeminence because you climbed onto your father's bed, and you defiled my bed by climbing onto it.
49.5: SIMEON and LEVI are brothers, their swords are instruments of violence.
49.6: May my soul not enter into their council. May my soul not join their assembly, for in their anger they slaughtered men, and in their fury they hamstrung bulls.
49.7: Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce, and their fury, for it was cruel. I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel.
49.8: You, Judah, your brothers will praise you, your hand will be on the neck of your enemies, your father's sons will bow down before you.
49.9: Judah is a young lion. You have risen from the slaughter, my son. He has crouches down, he has lain down like a lion, like a lioness: who will make him rouse himself?
49.10: The scepter will not depart from Judah, nor the ruler's staff from between his feet, until Shiloh comes; to him shall the peoples submit.
49.11: He ties his donkey to the vine, his donkey's colt to the vine, he washes his garment in wine, his cloak in the blood of the grape.
49.12: He has wine-red eyes and milk-white teeth.
49.13: Zebulun lives by the seashore, he is on the shore where ships land, his side is towards Sidon.
49.14: ISSACHAR is a robust donkey, who lies down in his paddocks.
49.15: He sees that rest is good, and that the land is pleasant, and he bends his shoulder under the burden; he has become a man enslaved to tribute.
49.16: DAN judges his people, as one of the tribes of Israel.
49.17: Dan is a snake on the road, a viper on the path, that bites the horse's heels, so that the rider falls backward.
49.18: I hope in your help, O Lord.
49.19: GAD is being pressured by armed gangs, and he, in turn, is pressing them on his heels.
49.20: D'ASER come The tasty bread, it provides the delicate dishes of kings.
49.21: NEPHTHALI is a doe in the wild, he speaks graceful words.
49.22: JOSEPH is the shoot of a fruitful tree, the shoot of a fruitful tree on the banks of a spring, its branches reach over the wall.
49.23: Archers provoke him, they shoot arrows at him and attack him.
49.24: But his bow remains steady, his arms and hands are made agile, by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, by him who is the Shepherd and the Rock of Israel.
49.25: May the God of your father help you and the Almighty bless you come to you the blessings of heaven above, the blessings of the abyss below, the blessings of the breasts and of the mother's womb.
49.26: The blessings of your father surpass the blessings of ancient mountains, the beauty of everlasting hills: may they rest upon the head of Joseph, upon the brow of the prince among his brothers
49.27: BENJAMIN is a wolf who tears, in the morning he devours the prey, in the evening he shares the spoils.
49.28: These were all the ones who formed the twelve tribes of Israel; this is what their father told them and blessed them. He blessed each one according to his blessing.
49.29: Then he gave them this order: I am about to be gathered to my people; bury me with my ancestors in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,
49.30: in the cave in the field of Machpelah, opposite Mamre, in the land of Canaan: it is the cave which Abraham acquired from Ephron the Hittite, along with the field, to have a tomb of his own.
49.31: This is where Abraham and Sarah, his wife, were buried; this is where Isaac and Rebecca, his wife, were buried; and this is where I buried Leah.
49.32: The field and the cave in it were acquired from the sons of Heth.
49.33: When Jacob had finished giving his instructions to his sons, and having withdrawn his feet into the bed, he breathed his last and was reunited with his fathers.

50.1: Joseph threw himself on his father's face, wept over him, and’ embrace has.
50.2: Then he ordered the doctors in his service to embalm his father, and the doctors embalmed Israel.
50.3: They spent forty days on it, for that is the time taken to embalm, and the Egyptians mourned him for seventy days.
50.4: When the days of his mourning were over, Joseph addressed the people of Pharaoh's house and said to them, "If I have found favor in your eyes, please report this to Pharaoh:
50.5: My father made me swear an oath, saying: “Behold, I am about to die, and you shall bury me in the tomb that I have dug for myself in the land of Canaan. I would like to go up there to bury my father, and I will return.”.
50.6: Pharaoh replied: Go up and bury your father, as he made you swear.
50.7: Joseph went up to bury his father. With him went all of Pharaoh’s servants, the elders of his house, and all the elders of the land of Egypt.
50.8: All of Joseph’s household, his brothers, and his father’s household: they left in the land of Gessen only their grandchildren, their sheep, and their oxen.
50.9: He was still going up with Joseph chariots and horsemen, so that the procession was very large.
50.10: When they arrived at the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, they made a great and deep lamentation, and Joseph observed a seven-day mourning period in honor of his father.
50.11: The inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, having seen this mourning at the threshing floor of Atad, said, “This is a great mourning among the Egyptians.” Therefore, the name of that place, which is beyond the Jordan, was given to that place, Abel-Mizraim.
50.12: The sons Jacob So they did to him as he had commanded them.
50.13: His sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah, which Abraham had acquired from Ephron the Hittite, with the field, to have a tomb of his own, opposite Mamre.
50.14: After burying his father, Joseph returned to Egypt with his brothers and all those who had gone up with him to bury his father.
50.15: When Joseph's brothers saw that their father was dead, they said, "What if Joseph were to hate us and repay us for all the harm we have done to him?"
50.16: And they sent word to Joseph: Your father gave this order before he died:
50.17: You shall say to Joseph, “Oh, forgive your brothers’ transgression and their sin, for they have wronged you. Now then, I beg you, forgive the transgression of the servants of the God of your father.” Joseph wept when he heard these words.
50.18: His brothers themselves came and prostrated themselves before him, saying: We are your servants.
50.19: Joseph said to them, “Do not be afraid, for am I in the place of God?”
50.20: You intended to harm me, but God intended to bring about good in all of this, to accomplish what is now being done, to preserve the lives of many.
50.21: So have no fear, I will provide for you and your children. And so he comforted them, speaking to their hearts.
50.22: Joseph remained in Egypt, he and his father's household. He lived one hundred and ten years.
50.23: Joseph saw the descendants of Ephraim to the third generation, descendants of Machir, the son of Manasseh, were born Also on Joseph's lap.
50.24: Joseph said to his brothers, “As for me, I am going to die, but God will surely visit you and bring you up out of this land into the land he swore to.” to give to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
50.25: Joseph made the Israelites swear an oath, saying, “Surely God will visit you, and you will bring up my bones from here.”.
50.26: Joseph died at the age of one hundred and ten. He was embalmed and placed in a coffin in Egypt.
